mechanism_narrative: Sustained acceleration in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects broad-based economic strength and corporate hiring, leading to higher expected future earnings and positive equity returns. The signal is based on the 3-month rate of change.
evidence_refs: fact:Payroll growth is a coincident indicator of economic activity.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward rank information coefficient (IC) remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a cyclical downturn and potential recession, leading to lower corporate profits and risk-off sentiment. This mechanism is only active when the rate rises sharply from its recent low.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE 3-month moving average rises > 0.3pp above its 12-month low.
failure_condition: In more than 40% of triggered instances, the S&P 500 posts positive returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: When the central bank is raising the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) while inflation (CPIAUCSL) is also accelerating, this 'hostile tightening' environment severely compresses equity valuation multiples and signals risk of a policy error, leading to negative returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal does not produce a statistically significant negative mean forward return (t-test p > 0.05) over the full backtest period.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime, strong growth in retail sales (RSAFS) is interpreted negatively by markets, as it signals persistent inflationary pressure that will force the central bank to tighten policy more aggressively, increasing discount rates.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month moving average.
failure_condition: The correlation between RSAFS growth and forward returns is not negative and statistically significant during periods where FEDFUNDS is above its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a slowdown in the manufacturing and industrial sectors, which are highly cyclical. This serves as a leading indicator for a broader economic downturn and lower corporate earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a key component of the Index of Coincident Economic Indicators.
failure_condition: The strategy of shorting the S&P 500 on a trigger event fails to generate a positive Sharpe ratio over a 10-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating housing starts (HOUST) signal weakening consumer confidence and construction activity, which has a strong multiplier effect on the broader economy. This leading indicator of demand destruction predicts negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Residential investment is a volatile and leading component of GDP.
failure_condition: The 6-month change in HOUST shows a positive or zero correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over the entire sample.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) creates a high 'risk-free' rate alternative to equities and increases the discount rate on future earnings, putting direct downward pressure on stock valuations. The effect is most pronounced over short horizons.
evidence_refs: fact:The Equity Risk Premium model uses the risk-free rate as a key input.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return when FEDFUNDS is above 4.0% is not statistically lower than when it is below 4.0% (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a very low unemployment rate (UNRATE) and decelerating real retail sales (RSAFS) signals a 'late-cycle' peak. It implies the labor market is tight but consumer spending power is waning, a precursor to an earnings recession.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is in the bottom quartile of its 5-year range AND RSAFS 6-month growth is negative.
failure_condition: Instances triggered by this joint condition are not followed by a negative average 60-day forward return over the full backtest period.
